pages.title: Red stop board (Sh 2)
pages.recognise: A red rectangular board with a white border.
pages.meaning: Stop because the line or track is closed or obstructed beyond the board.
doThis: Do not pass without explicit authority.

pages.title: Shunting limit board (Ra 10)
pages.recognise: A white semicircular board reading Halt für Rangierfahrten.
doThis: Stop the shunting movement before the board.

pages.title: Trapezoid board (Ne 1)
pages.recognise: A white trapezoid with a black border.
pages.meaning: A route-specific stop or reporting point used under defined operating procedures.
doThis: Follow the route or timetable instruction associated with the board.

pages.title: Distant signal board (Ne 2)
pages.recognise: A white rectangle with two black triangles meeting in the centre.
doThis: Read the accompanying warning and prepare for the next main signal.

pages.title: Speed announcement board (Lf 6)
pages.recognise: A yellow downward-pointing triangle with a black numeral.
pages.meaning: A lower speed is coming. Multiply the numeral by ten for km/h.
doThis: Reach the announced speed by the following Lf 7 or defined location.

pages.title: Speed board (Lf 7)
pages.recognise: A white rectangular board with a black numeral.
pages.meaning: The displayed speed applies from this point. Multiply the numeral by ten for km/h.
doThis: Be at or below the displayed speed when you reach the board.
